summary: Completed full backend + frontend E2E testing for Ecosafe / ecoprotec GmbH site. Backend: 9/9 pytest cases passed (health, kontakt valid/invalid-email/missing-fields/empty-strings, registrierung valid/invalid-email/missing-required, root). Telegram skipped=true as expected (keys intentionally empty). MongoDB persistence verified directly. Frontend: all 8 routes load with header/footer; Impressum contains HRB 3625, Markus Engemann, DE 812996130, Pamplonastraße 19, 33106 Paderborn; Kontakt form submit without datenschutz shows toast, with datenschutz shows 'Vielen Dank' toast and resets form; Registrierung form submit without datenschutz shows toast, with datenschutz navigates to success screen 'Vielen Dank für Ihre Bewerbung.'. One bug found: position pre-fill from Karriere to Registrierung does not work due to value mismatch.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking a job-card link (data-testid stelle-0..4) navigates to /registrierung/angestellte with location.state.position = e.g. 'Umweltgutachter:in (m/w/d)' (value includes the '(m/w/d)' suffix), but the POSITIONEN options in RegistrierungAngestellte.jsx are 'Umweltgutachter:in', 'ESG-Berater:in', etc. (without suffix). Because the <select> has no matching <option>, the pre-fill is silently discarded and the dropdown stays on 'Bitte wählen…'. Confirmed: after clicking stelle-0, r-position input_value is ''.

action_items: Fix position pre-fill mismatch in Karriere -> Registrierung flow. Two equally valid options: (a) in Karriere.jsx pass state={{ position: s.rolle.replace(' (m/w/d)', '') }} so it matches POSITIONEN, OR (b) update POSITIONEN in RegistrierungAngestellte.jsx to include the '(m/w/d)' suffix for all roles. Recommend option (a) to keep the dropdown concise.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py logger is configured AFTER send_telegram's module-level usage path — send_telegram references `logger` that is only defined at the bottom of the file. Because logger is only called inside functions at request time, this works today, but defining logger before `send_telegram` would be cleaner and safer against future refactors.

send_telegram catches broad Exception but returns the stringified error to clients in telegram.error. This is acceptable for this MVP but could leak internal info in production — consider sanitising in production.

Both contact and registration endpoints respond with HTTP 200 even when Telegram delivery fails (sent=false, skipped=false, error=...). That is correct for persistence-first design, but the API contract should be documented so the frontend knows to surface a partial-success state if needed.
MongoDB insert_one does not project _id out of the returned doc, but the endpoint only returns {ok,id,telegram} so the ObjectId never leaks to the client — good.
server.py is under 200 lines and fine; no split needed.
seed_data_creation: 3 test documents created in MongoDB (2 contact_messages, 1 employee_application) with TEST_ prefix. Safe to leave or cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is reusable. All 8 frontend routes confirmed rendering. Known open issue: position pre-fill from Karriere -> RegistrierungAngestellte is broken because job 'rolle' values include '(m/w/d)' suffix which is not in the POSITIONEN dropdown. After main agent fix, re-run: click [data-testid=stelle-0] on /karriere, then assert input_value of [data-testid=r-position] is non-empty and matches a POSITIONEN option. Telegram keys are intentionally empty — expected response is telegram.skipped=true; do NOT flag as failure.
rca_of_the_issue: Position pre-fill bug: Karriere.jsx line 51 passes state={{ position: s.rolle }} where s.rolle = 'Umweltgutachter:in (m/w/d)' etc. RegistrierungAngestellte.jsx reads location.state.position in useEffect and sets form.position. The <select data-testid='r-position'> has options from POSITIONEN array which contains bare role names without '(m/w/d)'. A <select> whose value does not match any <option>'s value stays visually on the first option (placeholder '') and onChange never fires, so the form.position effectively remains ''. Reproduction: navigate to /karriere, click [data-testid=stelle-0], on /registrierung/angestellte the dropdown shows 'Bitte wählen…'. Fix: normalize role string in Karriere.jsx state or add '(m/w/d)' variants to POSITIONEN.
